chunk_summary: Guidelines for escalating enteral feeding and inpatient care in children with CKD or CLD, focusing on energy intake, growth metrics, and electrolyte management thresholds.
learning_objectives: Understand thresholds for initiating enteral feeding in children with CKD or CLD.
Identify key growth and nutritional markers for intervention escalation.
Learn strategies to manage hyperkalemia and hyperphosphatemia in pediatric patients.
Recognize when inpatient care is necessary for monitoring and intervention.
key_facts: Escalate intervention if energy intake is <80% of the target for >10–14 days despite symptom control.
For children under 3 years with CKD, consider tube feeding if weight/length SDS ≤ -1.88 or growth is failing.
Prioritize MUAC/TSF over weight in cases of edema/ascites.
For CKD stages 3–5 or decompensated CLD, escalate energy/protein if height velocity stalls or a child crosses down ≥1 major percentile over 3–6 months.
For persistent hyperphosphatemia, avoid protein restriction; instead, adjust binder timing and remove phosphate additives.
Correct hyperkalemia by addressing constipation/acidosis and avoiding potassium additives; consider binders if dietary restrictions threaten adequacy.
reference_quote: ‘For children under 3 years with CKD, consider tube feeding when oral intake fails despite intervention and the child is underweight or growth retarded (weight or length/height ≤ -1.88 SDS) or is failing expected weight gain/growth.’ (KDOQI Clinical Practice Guidelines, 2008 Update)
document_key_topics: Enteral feeding thresholds in CKD and CLD
Growth and nutritional monitoring in pediatric patients
